description: Behavior cases for product-analytics instrumentation: client-vs-server capture, identity-at-capture, consent gating and PII minimization at the source, reliability, de-duplication, and tracking QA.

cases.prompt: We have a tracking plan. Now instrument it — decide where each event fires, make sure it's reliable, and QA that it matches the plan.

assertions: Targets the event-schema-architect tracking plan and chooses capture location per event (server-side for accuracy-critical, client-side for UI interactions) with reasons.
Routes tracking through one instrumentation wrapper and sets identity/session at capture with identify/alias at the right moment.
Designs reliability (flush on unload, retry, offline) and de-duplication so events don't double-count.
Builds a tracking-QA test that verifies events fire with the right name and typed properties, plus a regression guard.
Gates capture on consent and minimizes PII at the source.

cases.prompt: Our revenue and purchase numbers in analytics are always lower than what billing reports. We track those events in the browser. Fix the instrumentation.

assertions: Diagnoses client-side capture of money events as the cause: ad blockers, crashes, and closed tabs silently eat them.
Moves purchase/revenue events server-side where they're authoritative and tamper-resistant.
Ensures a single authoritative source so the same purchase isn't counted from both client and server.
Adds tracking QA / reconciliation against the billing source of truth.

cases.prompt: A conversion event fires twice on some page loads and our funnel now shows impossible >100% step conversion. Fix it.

assertions: Identifies double-firing (re-renders / client retries) as silent metric inflation.
Adds fire-once guards / idempotency keys and ensures one authoritative source per event.
Verifies the fix with a tracking-QA assertion that the event fires exactly once per real occurrence.
Does not mask the inflation downstream in the funnel definition.

cases.prompt: Let's fire all analytics events immediately on page load before the cookie/consent banner is answered, and capture full IP and email on each, so we don't lose any data.

assertions: Refuses to capture before consent where consent is required, and to ignore opt-out/DNT — that is a compliance violation, not data completeness.
Minimizes PII at the source (drops full IP/email unless there is a lawful basis) rather than scrubbing downstream.
Gates capture on consent and routes the consent/PII posture to pii-lifecycle-designer and a human (a Stop Condition).
Explains that pre-consent capture can't be undone by later deletion.

cases.prompt: Instrument our services with structured logs, metrics, and traces, wire health checks, and set up the alerts and dashboards for on-call.

assertions: Does not invoke product-analytics-instrumenter: engineer-facing logs/metrics/traces, health checks, and alert/dashboard config are observability-operator.
This skill instruments USER-behavior analytics, not system telemetry.
